revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hanashiyama-jinja]][[ja:羽梨山神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hanashiyama Shrine | native_name = 羽梨山神社 | image = Hanashiyama-jinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hanashiyama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.306766|140.246987}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hanashiyama Shrine'''|羽梨山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11481683|ltq=Q11481683|qid=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 25 ** P155: {{ill|Iharino Shrine|lt=Iharino Shrine|qid=Q135039769|1=Iharino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039770|ltq=Q135039770|qid=Q135039770|1=Nushiishino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q845851|ltq=Q845851|qid=Q845851|1=Kasama, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Hitachi Province|lt=Hitachi Province|cs|Provincie Hitači|de|Provinz Hitachi|en|Hitachi Province|es|Provincia de Hitachi|fa|ولایت هیتاچی|fr|Province de Hitachi|gl|Provincia de Hitachi|hak|Hitachi-koet|id|Provinsi Hitachi|it|Hitachi (provincia)|ja|常陸国|ko|히타치국|lt|Hitačio žemė|nl|Hitachi (provincie)|pt|Província de Province}} * {{ill|Q11617829|ltq=Q11617829|qid=Q11617829|1=Ibaraki district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.306766|140.246987}} == image (P18) == * Hanashiyama-jinja.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181778 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はなしやまじんじゃ == postal code (P281) == * 319-0201https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5050005002726 == Commons category (P373) == * Hanashiyama-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県笠間市上郷3161', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hanashiyama-jinja}} [[JA:羽梨山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66085126}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=Tuesday, July 30, 2019, 01:34 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Hanashiyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Hanashiyama-jinja.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = Kamigo 3161, Kasama City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha
Formerly a village shrine |Founded = 3rd year of Emperor Tenji (664) |Chief priest = |Main hall style = Irimoya-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = November 23 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Hanashiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Hanashiyama Shrine''' (はなしやまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Kamigo, Kasama City, Ibaraki Prefecture. The enshrined deity is Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto. It is a small shikinaisha of Hitachi Province, Ibaraki District, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === In ancient times, the shrine was located halfway up Mount Hanashi. In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji (664), a small shrine dedicated to Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto was built on the mountainside of Mount Hanashi, which was rich in cherry blossoms, and this led to the shrine being called “Kashirayama Shrine.”According to shrine tradition In the 22nd year of Enryaku (803), Sakanoue no Tamuramaro prayed here for victory in his campaign to subjugate Mutsu and donated shrine buildings.From the shrine precincts history monument (July 29, 2019) During the Jōhei–Tengyō Rebellion (the rebellion of Taira no Masakado), Taira no Sadamori dedicated bows, arrows, and gold dust and prayed here for victory in the subjugation of Taira no Masakado.According to shrine tradition In the Heian period, Minamoto no Yoriyoshi and Minamoto no Yoshiie dedicated spears, swords, armor, and sacred horses. Only the stirrups of the sacred horse still remain. In the Kamakura period, Shishido Ienari, who moved from Kamakura, rebuilt the shrine buildings.From the shrine precincts history monument (July 29, 2019) In the 11th year of Tenshō (1542), the shrine buildings on the mountainside of Mount Hanashi were destroyed by warfare, and the shrine was merged with Kumano Gongen at the mountain’s foothills, which became its current location. In the 8th year of Kanbun (1668), new shrine buildings were constructed. In the 16th year of Genroku (1703), the current Myōjin-style stone torii gate was erected, and in the 4th year of Enkyō (1747), the current shrine buildings were constructed. From the shrine precincts history monument (July 29, 2019) During the Meiji period, due to the Shinto–Buddhist Separation Edicts, the shrine was separated from the administration of its former bettō-ji, the temple Fugen-in. === Shrine rank === ==== Divine rank ==== Hanishi-no-kami * 12th year of Jōgan (870), August 23 – promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade“Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,” Volume 18, Jōgan 12 (870), August 28 * 1st year of Ninna (885), September 7 – promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade“Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,” Volume 48, Ninna 1 (885), September 7 ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== Hanashiyama Shrine * Meiji 6 (1868), April 1 – designated a village shrineFrom the shrine precincts history monument (July 29, 2019) * Meiji 40 (1902), May 20 – designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku from the government “Meiji Jinja Shiryō: Prefectural and Village Shrines, Volume 1,” Ibaraki Prefecture section, Nishi-Ibaraki District, Village Shrines ::Editor: Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office ::Publisher: Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office ::Publication date: Meiji 45 (1907) === Festivals === * New Year’s Festival (January 1) * Setsubun Festival (February 3) * Kinen Festival (April 11) * Grand Annual Festival (second Saturday and Sunday of November) * Niiname-sai (November 23)From the shrine precincts history monument (July 29, 2019) === Shrine precincts === File:Hanashiyama-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Hanashiyama-jinja Main hall.JPG|Main hall File:Hanashiyama-jinja Torii.JPG|Torii gate === Access === * 3.3 km from Iwama Station on the JR Jōban Line (about 40 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === See also === {{Commonscat}} {{Shinto sidebar}}{{List of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hanashiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hanashiyama Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:笠間市の建築物]] [[Category:笠間市の歴史]] [[Category:山岳信仰]]
